name: Saag Gosht- Beef or Lamb with Spinach - India
recipeIngredient: 4 inch piece fresh ginger root, coarsely chopped
2 2/3 lbs stewing beef or boned shoulder of lamb, cut into cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Microwave spinach until defrosted. Drain and squeeze out most of the water.
2. Put onion, ginger, and garlic in a food processor and pulse until finely chopped.
3. Heat oil in a pressure cooker over high heat.
4. Add bay leaves, cardamom, cloves, and cinnamon sticks to the hot oil and stir.
5. Add the finely chopped onion, ginger, and garlic mixture to the pot. Cook for 5 minutes over high heat.
6. Add meat, spinach, 2 cups water, salt, coriander, cumin, and cayenne pepper. Stir.
7. Secure the pressure cooker lid and bring it up to full pressure over high heat.
8. Once at full pressure, lower the heat and cook for the appropriate time (20 minutes for beef, 15 minutes for lamb).
9. Quickly cool the pressure cooker with cool water and remove the lid.
10. Add garam masala and return the contents to a boil.
11. Cook uncovered, stirring gently over high heat for 5-7 minutes, or until the sauce is reduced and thick.
